data.title: Sienna Senior Living reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Sienna Senior Living press release (OTCPK:LWSCF): Q2 Total Adjusted Revenue increased by 10.7% in Q2 2024 to $219.5 million, compared to Q2 2023.
  • Total NOI increased by 18.3% to $46.0 million, compared to Q2 2023.
  • OFFO per share increased by 21.4% in Q2 2024, or $0.063, to $0.357. The increase was primarily attributable to higher NOI.
  • AFFO per share increased by 14.6% in Q2 2024 to $0.307. The increase was primarily related to the increase in OFFO.

data.title: S&P 500’s biggest single-day gain proved buying the dip was a sound strategy - GS’s FICC and Equities
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) rose 2.3% in a single day, the biggest one-day gain since November 2022, closing at 5,254.

This means that “the $15B of S&P we currently have modeled out for the next five sessions from this community will be cut in half,” according to a Goldman Sachs’ FICC and Equities note.

Asset managers and hedge funds have bought this week’s weakness in higher quality defensives, and most of the demand was back today, even at higher prices.

Semiconductors (SMH), (SOXX) bounced back more than 6% today “after what felt like a growing chorus of frustration around the lingering beta/momentum that still exists in the A.I trade even about 30% off the highs,” the note said.

The GLP-1 drug trade is the second most crowded, with Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY) up 9% after a huge EPS beat driven by sales and margins.

From here, market moves will go “choppy but higher,” analysts said. “Buying 5% dips in the S&P 500 (SP500) has proven to be a very sound strategy over time.”

data.title: Heritage Insurance stock jumps after Piper upgrades to Overweight
data.description:
Heritage Insurance Holdings (NYSE:HRTG) shares accelerated 17% in Friday morning trading after Piper Sandler upgraded the property and casualty insurer to Overweight from Neutral just days after Heritage posted stronger-than-expected Q2 earnings and revenue.

"We see its renewed efforts to grow as a sign that HRTG has turned the corner fully to rate adequacy and the home insurance market is ready for earnings growth," analyst Paul Newsome wrote in a note.

Following multiple years of price increases and and more recent moderating claims inflation, Heritage (HRTG) "now appears to be solidly profitable," he added.

His Outperform rate aligns with the SA Quant system rating of Strong Buy and the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.
